title: MirrorTrace Public-Archive Self-Exposure Audit Input
description: Finds PUBLIC web-archive (Wayback) snapshots of a URL/host you own — including pages you have since deleted — so you can request removal. Prohibited and non-applicable scopes are absent from the enum: only self/public_figure are accepted because enumerating an archive prefix is a dual-use technique restricted to those scopes. Only public archived snapshots of YOUR OWN surface are read; there is no field for third-party identity, romance, gender, location, or social-graph data.

scope_type.title: Scope type
scope_type.description: Archive enumeration is permitted ONLY for your own footprint (self) or a genuine public figure acting publicly. Other compliant scopes (consented/brand/safety_evidence) intentionally cannot run this actor.

subject_url.title: Your URL or host
subject_url.description: A URL or host you OWN (or a public figure's official site), e.g. https://example.com or example.com/blog. The Wayback CDX index is queried for archived snapshots under this prefix.

match_prefix.title: Match the whole prefix
match_prefix.description: If true (default), every archived URL beginning with your subject_url is listed. If false, only the exact URL is checked.

max_snapshots.title: Max snapshots
max_snapshots.description: Upper bound on archived snapshots to retrieve from the CDX index (deduped by content digest). Keeps the run bounded and polite.

scan_snapshot_pii.title: Scan snapshot text for your PII
scan_snapshot_pii.description: If true, each PUBLIC snapshot is fetched and the SHARED PII detector is run over its text to flag your own email/phone/handle still exposed in a deleted page. Off by default (listing is cheaper). Never fabricates a hit.

subject_label.title: Subject label
subject_label.description: Human label for the subject (e.g. your name). Used only for reporting.
